text.title: AI Sprint Planning Agent Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Sprint Planning Agent application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apply to a single team's sprint plan. Cross-team rollups require `cross-team-estimation-normalisation`.
rule.rationale: Cross-team mixing destroys per-team velocity comparability.

rule.statement: Velocity, calendar, carryover, focus-time are all typed inputs with source paths. Free-form 'about 50 points' = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs produce untyped (and unfalsifiable) plans.

rule.statement: Capacity output carries a confidence band (e.g. 30-45 story points) not a single point estimate.
Carryover work is added to the proposed sprint at the top of the plan; never silently dropped or hidden.
rule.rationale: Hidden carryover is how teams lie to themselves about velocity.

rule.statement: Every agent recommendation cites a specific velocity sprint, OOO entry, or carryover item. No ungrounded recommendations.
Sprint plan carries semver + sprint id. Re-runs after material changes are new versions.
rule.rationale: Plans evolve; versions make the evolution legible.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
